prompt: The person pick up the hammer from the table, uses their right hand to hammer some nails on the wall.
The person, with their right hand, picks up the elephant from a high table using their right hand, passes it to someone across the table.
Working exclusively with the right hand, the person pick up a wine glass from the table, takes a drink from it.
Only with the right hand, the person picks up a water bottle from the lower table with their right hand, raises it to ear level, vigorously shakes it several times.
Using only their right hand, the person picks up the watch from the table with their right hand, raises it to shoulder level on their right side.
The person uses only their right hand to picks up the alarm clock with their right hand, examines it at neck level with their right hand.

With their right hand, they picks up the apple from a high table with their right hand, passes it across the table at shoulder level with their right hand, and places it back on the table with their right hand.
Keeping to the right hand, the person picks up the camera from the high table, presses some buttons to browse the camera's context.
The person, with their right hand, pick up the medium cube from the table, holds it closer for examination.
With their right hand, they picks up the duck with their right hand, examines it closely with a keen eye.
The person, using their right hand, repeatedly pick up and put down a game controller from a table.
The person uses only their right hand to picks up the phone using the right hand and dials a number, starts talking on the phone while walking.
With their right hand, they pick up the Stanford bunny from the table, briefly turns to the right while holding it for inspection.

Using the right hand throughout, the person retrieves a medium sphere from a high table with their right hand, holds it for a close examination, and and then repositions it on the table using the same hand.
The person, with their right hand, picks up the medium torus from the table, inspects and rotates it at eye level with their right hand.
Keeping to the right hand, the person picks up and puts down the binoculars on the table with their right hand.
